etymology.description: Semantic 舁(舁) (yú, "carry") + phonetic 亼(亼) (jí, "gather") -> giving or joining -> and
etymology.interpretation: The image of carrying and gathering stems from 舁(舁) (yú, "carry") as the semantic component, suggesting support, while 亼(亼) (jí, "gather") contributes the phonetic sound. This combination evolves into the notion of inclusion or joining, leading naturally to the meaning 'and'.
etymology.simplification: 与 is a simplified form, reducing 與(與) by removing the internal components and simplifying the structure for easier writing.

examples.chinese: 我与朋友一起去公园(我與朋友一起去公園)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ yǔ péngyǒu yīqǐ qù gōngyuán.
examples.english: I went to the park with friends.

examples.chinese: 他与她都是医生(他與她都是醫生)。
examples.pinyin: Tā yǔ tā dōu shì yīshēng.
examples.english: He and she are both doctors.

examples.chinese: 这朵花与那朵花颜色相似(這朵花與那朵花顏色相似)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè duǒ huā yǔ nà duǒ huā yánsè xiāngsì.
examples.english: This flower and that flower have similar colors.
